candidate.name: InP substrates
candidate.description: Qualified upstream indium phosphide substrate supply for AI photonics and optical networking.
notes: Highest concentration and geopolitical-exposure profile in the chain.
Important as a fragility node, but not necessarily the best value-capture layer.

score.explanation: InP substrates scores 86.0/100 (critical) as a chokepoint candidate. The strongest weighted drivers are supplier concentration, geopolitical exposure, qualification friction. The least supportive signals are pricing power evidence, substitutability inverse. This score should be treated as a ranking aid, not as a trading decision on its own.

candidate.name: InP laser and external light source capacity
candidate.description: Qualified InP laser and external light source capacity for co-packaged optics and silicon photonics.
notes: This layer scores nearly as high as raw substrates.
A better value-capture candidate is plausible here because customer qualification and device economics may matter more than upstream scarcity purity.

score.candidate_name: InP laser and external light source capacity
score.explanation: InP laser and external light source capacity scores 83.2/100 (critical) as a chokepoint candidate. The strongest weighted drivers are supplier concentration, qualification friction, demand acceleration. The least supportive signals are pricing power evidence, geopolitical exposure. This score should be treated as a ranking aid, not as a trading decision on its own.

candidate.name: Optical module and CPO integration
candidate.description: System-level co-packaged optics and optical module integration for hyperscaler AI networks.
notes: This layer is important, but less extreme as a pure bottleneck than substrate or qualified laser capacity.
Value capture may still be strong here even if scarcity is less severe.

score.explanation: Optical module and CPO integration scores 69.2/100 (high) as a chokepoint candidate. The strongest weighted drivers are qualification friction, demand acceleration, supplier concentration. The least supportive signals are pricing power evidence, geopolitical exposure. This score should be treated as a ranking aid, not as a trading decision on its own.
